alternative_parents.description: Inorganic compounds in which the largest metallic oxoanion is aluminate, to which either no atom or a non metal atom is bonded.
alternative_parents.name: Miscellaneous aluminates

alternative_parents.description: Inorganic compounds consisting of an assembly of cations and anions.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic salts

alternative_parents.description: Inorganic chemical compounds with one or more oxygen atoms combined with another element.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic oxides

class.description: Inorganic oxoanionic compounds in which the heaviest atom not in an oxoanion is an alkaline earth metal.
class.name: Alkaline earth metal oxoanionic compounds

description: This compound belongs to the class of inorganic compounds known as alkaline earth metal aluminates. These are inorganic compounds in which the largest oxoanion is aluminate, and in which the heaviest atom not in an oxoanion is a lanthanide.

direct_parent.description: Inorganic compounds in which the largest oxoanion is aluminate, and in which the heaviest atom not in an oxoanion is a lanthanide.
direct_parent.name: Alkaline earth metal aluminates

kingdom.description: Compounds that do not contain a C atom. Exceptions are made for isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon trioxide, carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Inorganic compounds

subclass.description: Inorganic compounds in which the largest oxoanion is aluminate, and in which the heaviest atom not in an oxoanion is a lanthanide.
subclass.name: Alkaline earth metal aluminates

superclass.description: Compounds that contains both metal and non metal atoms.
superclass.name: Mixed metal/non-metal compounds
